title: bromeliaceae
intro: The bromeliaceae rank is a family of plants, specifically a group of monocots in the order Poales. This family, commonly known as the bromeliad family, consists of approximately 3,000 species of epiphytes, which are plants that grow on other surfaces such as trees, rocks, and even power lines. The bromeliaceae family is characterized by its unique structure, which includes a central cup that collects and stores water, allowing the plants to survive in dry environments. Many species within this family are popular ornamental plants, prized for their vibrant flowers and attractive foliage.
